title: NDIC Begins Liquidation of Aso Savings, Union Homes After CBN License Revocation - FinancialEDGE
description: The Nigeria Deposit Insurance Corporation (NDIC) has commenced the liquidation process for Aso Savings and Loans Plc and Union Homes Savings and Loans Plc, following the revocation of their licenses by the Central Bank of Nigeria (CBN) on December 15, 2025. html:
The Nigeria Deposit Insurance Corporation (NDIC) has commenced the liquidation process for Aso Savings and Loans Plc and Union Homes Savings and Loans Plc, following the revocation of their licenses by the Central Bank of Nigeria (CBN) on December 15, 2025.

Depositors will receive up to ₦2 million in insured deposits, with the NDIC using the Bank Verification Number (BVN) to locate alternate bank accounts for automatic credit.

Those with higher balances will receive the initial ₦2 million and await liquidation dividends from asset sales and debt recovery.

Depositors can submit claims online via (link unavailable) or visit closed bank branches between December 16-30, 2025, with required documents including proof of account ownership and BVN.

For further information or clarification on claims verification and payments, depositors and other stakeholders may contact the Corporation through the following channels: E-mail: claimscomplaints@ndic.gov.ngClaims Resolution Department: (Weekdays: 9:00 a.m. to 5:00 p.m.) Telephone Numbers: 09037273810, 09038197064, 08109313326, 08104220807, 09064657140 Or contact: The Director Claims Resolution Department Nigeria Deposit Insurance Corporation Lagos Office, 8th Floor, Mamman Kontagora House23A, Marina P.M.B. 12881, Lagos.
